chapter_title: Vegetable plaiting materials; vegetable products not elsewhere specified or included
inclusions: Bamboos (whether or not split, sawn lengthwise, cut to length, rounded at ends, bleached, rendered non-inflammable, polished or dyed)
Rattans (whole, split-core, split-skin) and drawn or split rattans
Split osier, reeds and the like
Vegetable materials of a kind used primarily for plaiting: raffia, cleaned/bleached/dyed cereal straw, lime bark
Other vegetable products NES: kapok, palm kernel shells, empty fruit bunch of oil palm, materials used primarily in tanning or dyeing

exclusions.condition: Vegetable materials used PRIMARILY in TEXTILE manufacture. (NOTE: Plaiting materials like bamboo or rattan for weaving/plaiting are NOT textiles and belong to Chapter 14)
Prepared knots or tufts for broom or brush making

classification_rules.rule: Key boundary: Ch.14 (vegetable plaiting materials) vs Ch.57 (carpets) vs Ch.46 (basketware)
classification_rules.description: Chapter 14 = raw vegetable materials suitable for plaiting: osier, bamboo, rattan, rushes, straw, raffia in primary/unworked form. Woven or plaited articles made FROM these → Ch.46 (basketware, matwork). Floor coverings made from plaiting materials → Ch.57.

classification_rules.rule: Ch.14 vs Ch.06 (live plants) vs Ch.44 (wood)
classification_rules.description: Vegetable fibre/plaiting material in raw form → Ch.14. Living plant → Ch.06. Wood (even bamboo timber) → Ch.44. Bamboo as raw plaiting material (strips, canes) → Ch.14. Structural bamboo products → Ch.44. The form of presentation and end use determines the chapter.
